review_notes: The functions generally map well to the NIST AC-6(4) control, focusing on least privilege and separation of processing domains. The RBAC and Pod Security functions are well-named and technically accurate. The NetworkPolicy function needed a naming adjustment for clarity and to better reflect the action of isolating domains.
naming_improvements: Use 'isolate' instead of 'separate' to clearly indicate the action of isolating network traffic between domains
title: Least Privilege - Least privilege | separate processing domains provide separate processing domain
step1_reasoning: The control AC-6(4) emphasizes enforcing least privilege and ensuring separate processing domains. In Kubernetes, this can be addressed through RBAC by ensuring roles and bindings enforce least privilege, NetworkPolicies to isolate network traffic between different domains, and Pod Security Standards to restrict privileges and ensure separation at the pod level.
